LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF NIKKI BAKER'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER DETERMINING RESTITUTION PURSUANT TO DEFENDANTS 998 OFFER. CONTINUED TO JULY 10, 2019. THE PARTIES ARE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER IMMEDIATELY BY PHONE OR IN PERSON REGARDING THE APPROPRIATE AMOUNT OF RESTITUTION PER CIVIL CODE 1793.2(D)(2)(B) AND OTHER DAMAGES PER CIVIL CODE 1794. IF A DISPUTE EXISTS AFTER THE PARTIES MEANINGFULLY MEET AND CONFER, EACH SIDE MAY FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF NO LONGER THAN 10 PAGES SETTING FORTH THE DISPUTE AND THE PARTIES' RESPECTIVE POSITIONS BY JULY 1, 2019. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

Defendant Fca Us Llcs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs
Off Calendar Sep-16-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JOHN P. MYERS, Esq. of the Bickel Law Firm, Inc. (Tel: 619-374-4100) for plaintiff;
Atty. BRETT H. WANNER, Esq. of Universal & Shannon, LLP (Tel: 916-780-4050) for Defendant FCA US LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT FCA US LLC's Motion To Tax Costs.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

efendant FCA's motion to tax costs is denied. By its CCP section 998 offer to compromise, which plaintiff accepted, FCA agreed to pay plaintiff "a sum equal to the aggregate amount of costs and expenses, including attorney's fees based on actual time reasonably incurred in connection with the commencement and prosecution of this action pursuant to Civil Code Section 1794(b), to be determined by court motion if the parties cannot agree." Under Civ. Code Sec. 1794(d), the prevailing buyer in a Song-Beverly Act action "shall be allowed by the court to recover as part of the judgment a sum equal to the aggregate amount of costs and expenses, including attorney's bees based on actual time expended, determined by the court to have been reasonably incurred by the buyer in connection with the commencement and prosecution of such action." The Legislature thus intended the prevailing buyer in an action under the Song-Beverly Act to recover both costs and expenses, which is broader than the detailed statutory definition of costs contained in Code of Civil Procedure section 1033.5. (Warren v. Kia Motors America, Inc. (2018) 30 Cal.App.4th 24, 42 [trial court erroneously granted motion to tax cost of court reporter trial transcripts]; Jensen v. BMW of North America, Inc. (1995) 35 Cal.App.4th 112, 137-138.) FCA, which does not support its motion with any admissible evidence, does not meet its burden to show that the challenged costs were not reasonably incurred. (See Jones v. Dumrichob (1998) 63 Cal.App.4th 1258, 1266 ["If items on their face appear to be proper charges, the verified memorandum of costs is prima facie evidence of their propriety, and the burden is on the party seeking to tax costs to show they were not reasonable or necessary."]; see also Ladas v. California State Automobile Ass'n (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 761, 774.) The court finds, based upon the Myers declaration submitted in opposition to the motion, that all of the costs sought by plaintiff were reasonably incurred in connection with the commencement and prosecution of the action.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Determining Restitution Pursuant To Defendants 998 Offer; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of David C. Mcgaffey; Exhibits A-H; [Proposed] Order
Off Calendar Jul-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
David McGaffey, esq. (619-374-4100) of Bickel Law Firm, Inc. for Plaintiff, Nikki Baker via Court Call.
Nejla Nassirian, esq. (916-780-4050) of Universal & Shannon, LLP for Defendant, FCA US LLC via Court Call.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF NIKKI BAKER's Motion For An Order Determining Restitution Pursuant To Defendants 998 Offer.

Plaintiff Nikki Baker's motion for an order determining restitution pursuant to Defendant FCA US LLC's 998 offer is granted in the amount of $28,003.83. Pursuant to Civil Code 1793.2(d)(2)(C), the mileage offset is to be determined on the basis of "the number of miles traveled by the new motor vehicle prior to the time the buyer first delivered the vehicle to the manufacturer or distributor, or its authorized service and repair facility for correction of the problem that gave rise to the nonconformity." Here, that calculation is to be performed as of October 27, 2016, when Plaintiff reported problems with the transmission. (McGaffey Decl., Ex. BB.) Plaintiff's vehicle registration renewal fees are recoverable as incidental and consequential damages. (See Ruiz v. BMW of North America, LLC (C.D. Cal. May 7, 2018) 2018 WL 2106454, at *5-6.) Defendant's reliance on Kirzhner v. Mercedes-Benz USA, LLC (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 453, review granted, No. S246444 (Feb. 21, 2018) is misplaced, as that decision has no binding or precedential effect while it is pending review in the California Supreme Court. (See Cal. R. Ct. 8.1115(e)(1).)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
